title: TRANSITIONAL LIVING MATERNITY PROGRAM - 211info
text: INTEGRAL YOUTH SERVICES TRANSITIONAL LIVING MATERNITY PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides long-term supportive housing, including case management, parenting and life skills support, access to healthcare, prenatal services, childcare resources, and community programs to help young parents move toward stability and independence. Participants must be willing to voluntarily engage in the program, follow house guidelines, and work with staff on goals related to stability, parenting, education, or employment. 115 N 10th Street Klamath Falls, OR 97601 Eligibility: Women ages 18-24 who are pregnant or parenting and experiencing homelessness, at risk of homelessness, or living in unsafe or unstable housing Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 882-2053 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (541) 882-2053 Main phone Main phone (541) 882-2053 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Walk in, call, or email to complete an intake interview and needs assessment to determine eligibility, safety, and appropriate placement FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None AREA SERVED: Klamath County SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Located on the corner of 10th Street and Main Street. BTS bus stop is 2 blocks away. Services: Family Permanent Supportive Housing Maternity Homes Transitional Housing/Shelter * Women If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. INTEGRAL YOUTH SERVICES
